evals.prompt: I want to start a new side project called recipe-box. It's a recipe organizer app. Set up the project for me.
evals.expected_output: Uses the scaffold script, correct conventions, defers Firebase

assertions.text: Uses new-project.sh scaffold script instead of running tanstack create directly or scaffolding manually
Project name is recipe-box
Does not install Tailwind, Sass, CSS-in-JS, Redux, Zustand, Jotai, React Hook Form, SWR, or Axios
Defers Firebase project setup — does not create a Firebase project, set up DNS, or run firebase apphosting:backends:create unless the user explicitly asked

evals.prompt: I need a bookmarks feature for my app. Users can save URLs with a title and tags, and see them in a list. Add the feature with routes, the page component, and a Firestore-backed data layer.
evals.expected_output: Creates features/bookmarks/ with data.ts + types.ts + components/, thin route file in routes/, Zod schemas, useSuspenseQuery + loader, locked Firestore rules

assertions.text: Feature lives in src/features/bookmarks/ with data.ts, types.ts, and components/
Route file in src/routes/ contains createFileRoute config only — no JSX in the route file
Uses kebab-case filenames (e.g. bookmarks-page.tsx) with PascalCase exports (BookmarksPage)
types.ts uses Zod schemas with z.infer for TypeScript types
data.ts exports a query key factory, queryOptions function, and at least one createServerFn for data access
Page component uses useSuspenseQuery; route loader prefetches via queryClient.ensureQueryData
Server function imports from ~/lib/firebase-admin (Admin SDK), not ~/lib/firebase (client SDK)
Includes Firestore security rules with auth-owns-data pattern (request.auth.uid match), not open rules
No barrel files (no index.ts re-exports)

evals.prompt: Add Firebase Auth with Google sign-in to my app. Lock dashboard routes behind auth.
evals.expected_output: Router context-based auth with beforeLoad guard, no React Context provider, createRootRouteWithContext

assertions.text: Auth state lives in TanStack Router context via createRootRouteWithContext, not in a React Context provider
_authed/route.tsx uses beforeLoad to redirect when context.user is null — does not check auth in a component or useEffect
client.tsx subscribes to onAuthStateChanged and calls router.invalidate() (or equivalent) on user change
Components read user via useRouteContext() from a route, not via a useAuth hook backed by React Context
Uses signInWithPopup with GoogleAuthProvider from firebase/auth (not @angular/fire, not a custom Auth class)

evals.prompt: My project mood-tracker is ready to go live. Set up Firebase App Hosting, configure the subdomain mood-tracker.charlies.bot, and deploy it.
evals.expected_output: Uses App Hosting, apphosting.yaml with VITE_FIREBASE_* BUILD vars, gcloud MCP for DNS, charlies-bot zone, git-push deploy

assertions.text: Uses Firebase App Hosting (not classic firebase deploy --only hosting)
Creates or references apphosting.yaml; VITE_FIREBASE_* env vars use availability: [BUILD]
Uses run_gcloud_command MCP tool for DNS setup in the charlies-bot zone
Uses firebase apphosting:backends:create CLI for backend setup (Firebase MCP does not support App Hosting backend creation)
Warns the user that backends:create opens the browser for GitHub connection via Developer Connect before running it
Deploys via git push to the connected branch (not firebase deploy for hosting)

evals.prompt: I need an API endpoint that takes a URL and returns the page title and meta description. Add it.
evals.expected_output: Uses TanStack server function (createServerFn), not Cloud Function or separate functions/ directory

assertions.text: Uses createServerFn from @tanstack/start (not a Cloud Function, not a separate API route)
Validates input with .validator(zSchema) — Zod schema for the URL input
Does not create a separate functions/ directory or add firebase-functions dependency
Does not suggest firebase deploy --only functions for this endpoint
Server function lives in src/features/<name>/data.ts (or src/lib/ if cross-feature), not co-located in a route file
